---
title: cooperbench run
description: Run agents on CooperBench tasks
---

The `cooperbench run` command executes agents on benchmark tasks. It supports both cooperative (multi-agent) and solo (single-agent) settings.

## Usage

```bash
cooperbench run [options]
```

## Basic examples

### Run with auto-generated name

```bash
cooperbench run --setting solo -s lite
```

Auto-generates name like: `solo-msa-gemini-3-flash-lite`

### Run with custom name

```bash
cooperbench run -n my-experiment --setting solo -r llama_index_task
```

Runs experiment named `my-experiment` on llama_index repository tasks.

### Multi-agent cooperative setting

```bash
cooperbench run -n team-experiment --setting coop -s lite --git
```

Runs two agents cooperatively with git collaboration enabled.

## Parameters

### Experiment identification

<ParamField path="-n, --name" type="string">
  Experiment name. If not provided, auto-generated from settings.
  
  **Format:** `{setting}-{agent}-{model}-{subset}-{repo}-{task}`
  
  **Examples:** `solo-msa-gemini-3-flash`, `coop-sw-git-gpt-4o-lite`
</ParamField>

### Task filtering

<ParamField path="-s, --subset" type="string">
  Use a predefined task subset. See `dataset/subsets/` for available subsets.
  
  **Common values:** `lite`, `full`
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="-r, --repo" type="string">
  Filter by repository name.
  
  **Example:** `llama_index_task`, `dspy_task`
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="-t, --task" type="integer">
  Filter by specific task ID.
  
  **Example:** `8394`, `1234`
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="-f, --features" type="string">
  Specific feature pair to run, comma-separated.
  
  **Example:** `1,2` (runs features 1 and 2)
</ParamField>

### Model and agent

<ParamField path="-m, --model" type="string" default="vertex_ai/gemini-3-flash-preview">
  LLM model to use. Supports any LiteLLM-compatible model string.
  
  **Examples:**
  - `vertex_ai/gemini-3-flash-preview`
  - `gpt-4o`
  - `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
  - `anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514`
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="-a, --agent" type="string" default="mini_swe_agent">
  Agent framework to use.
  
  **Options:**
  - `mini_swe_agent` - Lightweight SWE-agent (default)
  - `swe_agent` - Full SWE-agent
  - `openhands` - OpenHands agent
  - Custom agents via `COOPERBENCH_EXTERNAL_AGENTS` environment variable
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="--agent-config" type="string">
  Path to agent-specific configuration file. Format depends on the agent.
  
  **Example:** `--agent-config configs/custom.yaml`
  
  See [Agent configuration](/cli/agent-config) for details.
</ParamField>

### Execution settings

<ParamField path="--setting" type="choice" default="coop">
  Benchmark setting.
  
  **Options:**
  - `coop` - Two agents working cooperatively (default)
  - `solo` - Single agent working alone
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="-c, --concurrency" type="integer" default="30">
  Number of parallel tasks to run.
  
  **Default:** 30
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="--backend" type="choice" default="modal">
  Execution backend.
  
  **Options:**
  - `modal` - Modal cloud platform (default)
  - `docker` - Local Docker containers
  - `gcp` - Google Cloud Platform VMs
  
  See [Backends](/cli/backends) for configuration details.
</ParamField>

### Collaboration features

<ParamField path="--redis" type="string" default="redis://localhost:6379">
  Redis URL for inter-agent communication in cooperative mode.
  
  **Default:** `redis://localhost:6379`
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="--git" type="flag">
  Enable git collaboration. Agents can push/pull/merge via shared remote.
  
  When enabled, each agent gets their own branch and can share code via a "team" remote.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="--no-messaging" type="flag">
  Disable the `send_message` command for agent communication.
</ParamField>

### Evaluation

<ParamField path="--no-auto-eval" type="flag">
  Disable automatic evaluation after task completion.
  
  By default, evaluation runs automatically after all tasks finish.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="--eval-concurrency" type="integer" default="10">
  Number of parallel evaluations for auto-eval.
  
  **Default:** 10
</ParamField>

### Other options

<ParamField path="--force" type="flag">
  Force rerun even if results already exist in `logs/` directory.
</ParamField>

## Filtering examples

### Run specific subset

```bash
cooperbench run -s lite
```

### Run specific repository

```bash
cooperbench run -r llama_index_task
```

### Run specific task

```bash
cooperbench run -t 8394
```

### Run specific feature pair

```bash
cooperbench run -t 8394 -f 1,2
```

Runs task 8394, features 1 and 2 only.

### Combine filters

```bash
cooperbench run -s lite -r dspy_task --setting solo
```

Runs lite subset, dspy repository only, solo mode.

## Backend examples

### Run on Modal (cloud)

```bash
cooperbench run --backend modal
```

Default cloud execution on Modal.

### Run locally with Docker

```bash
cooperbench run --backend docker
```

Runs tasks in local Docker containers.

### Run on GCP

```bash
cooperbench run --backend gcp
```

Requires prior configuration with `cooperbench config gcp`.

## Model examples

### OpenAI models

```bash
cooperbench run -m gpt-4o
cooperbench run -m gpt-4o-mini
```

Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable.

### Anthropic models

```bash
cooperbench run -m claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022
cooperbench run -m anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514
```

Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable.

### Google models

```bash
cooperbench run -m vertex_ai/gemini-3-flash-preview
cooperbench run -m gemini/gemini-pro
```

Requires `GEMINI_API_KEY` or GCP authentication for Vertex AI.

## Collaborative settings

### Git collaboration

```bash
cooperbench run --setting coop --git
```

Enables shared git remote. Agents can:
- Push to `team` remote: `git push team agent_0`
- Fetch teammates' work: `git fetch team`
- View changes: `git log team/agent_1`

### Messaging only

```bash
cooperbench run --setting coop
```

Agents use `send_message` command to coordinate.

### No collaboration

```bash
cooperbench run --setting coop --no-messaging
```

Agents work independently, merge at the end.

## Output

Results are saved to:

```
logs/{experiment_name}/
  task_{task_id}_feature_{f1}_{f2}/
    agent_0/
      trajectory.jsonl
      patch.diff
    agent_1/
      trajectory.jsonl  
      patch.diff
    eval.json
```

## Auto-generated names

When `--name` is omitted, names follow this pattern:

```
{setting}-{agent_short}-{git?}-{model}-{subset?}-{repo?}-{task?}
```

**Examples:**
- `solo-msa-gemini-3-flash` - Solo, mini_swe_agent, Gemini 3 Flash
- `solo-msa-gemini-3-flash-lite` - Same but lite subset
- `coop-sw-git-gpt-4o-dspy-8394` - Coop, swe_agent, git enabled, GPT-4o, dspy repo, task 8394

**Agent shorthands:**
- `msa` = mini_swe_agent
- `sw` = swe_agent
- `oh` = openhands

See `cooperbench/agents/__init__.py` for full list.
